title: Reference to an other sheet in formula
author: Orelsanpls
body:
Hi, I have a problem using formula and Sheet reference.

I am using this :  
```

sheet.getCell('A2').dataValidation = {
          type: 'list',
          allowBlank: true,
          formulae: ['Company.A2:A500'],
          showInputMessage: true,
          promptTitle: 'Company',
          prompt: 'In which company the user is in.',
};
```

And when I generate the XLSX and open the file with _libre office_ I get **'company.a2:A500'** in data validation and it don't work.

I guess there is a bug in Sheet name handling, at some point you seems to put some data in lowercase.

If you have a patch I take it please! :)

**PS**: I did try this too, and it gives the same behavior

```
sheet.getCell('A2').value = {
        formula: 'Company.A10',
        value: '',
};
```

comments.author: Orelsanpls
comments.body:
If you hit the same trouble, use the [names functionality](https://github.com/guyonroche/exceljs#defined-names), it will permit you to avoid the bug :)

Enjoy!

comments.author: abhishek-raj
comments.body:
I faced the same issue, went through the most part of the code of exceljs trying to fix it, seems it correctly generates the xml.

I corrected it by using (!) instead of (.)

Eg.

sheet.getCell('A2').dataValidation = {
          type: 'list',
          allowBlank: true,
          formulae: ['Company!A2:A500'],
          showInputMessage: true,
          promptTitle: 'Company',
          prompt: 'In which company the user is in.',
};

Excel just shows a . but we need to use ! instead.

comments.author: erixtekila
comments.body:
@GregoryNEUT Would you mind giving an example, please ?
I can't get to use cell names in formulas.

comments.author: erixtekila
kaykayehnn
akomm
comments.body:
This is a really nasty bug, because if you wrap the reference to a named range in a function, it breaks again.

Works via name: `=some.custom.name`
Breaks again: `=IFNA(some.custom.name; 0)`

In the generated XLSX I get #name, when I focus into the cell, remove the `=` and type `=` in again, then submit, the formula starts to work. Same behavior when referencing via sheet name (`$sheetname.addr`).

So yes, naming helps reference the value, but when you want to wrap it into a function it breaks again.

Currently workaround verifying the other sheet has a reference by running something like that:

```ts
const rangeNames = sheet.workbook.definedNames.model.map(v => v.name) // or .filter, depends on other code
if (rangeNames.includes("foo.bar") && rangeNames.includes("bar.foo")) {
  // ..
}
```

comments.author: mortenhillbom
comments.body:
@akomm have you tried using comma instead of semicolon in the function? `=IFNA(some.custom.name, 0)`
I had a similar problem with functions, that was unrelated to the reference, but this fixed it.

comments.author: akomm
comments.body: I actually initially used comma, but it created files that excel couldn't open. It only works with semi.
